channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19363] IPv6 sanity-lnet test_270: FAIL: Didn't find metadata reply message in log
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for jianyu <yujian@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/78dc0768-5f14-4dfc-bf72-2314eee77756

test_270 failed with the following error:

Loading LNet and configuring DLC
Loading modules from /usr/lib64/lustre
detected 2 online CPUs by sysfs
MODOPTS_LIBCFS=
Force libcfs to create 2 CPU partitions
../libcfs/libcfs/libcfs options: 'cpu_npartitions=2'
../lnet/lnet/lnet options: 'accept=all'
/usr/sbin/lnetctl lnet configure -l
fail_loc=0x1000e003
fail_val=100
/usr/sbin/lnetctl ping fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:2d:e5f3@tcp
ping:
- primary nid: fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:2d:e5f3@tcp
  Multi-Rail: false
  peer_ni:
  - nid: fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:2d:e5f3@tcp
 sanity-lnet test_270: @@@@@@ FAIL: Didn't find metadata reply message in log

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4652 - 5.14.0-570.37.1.el9_6.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-master/4652 - 5.14.0-570.37.1_lustre.el9.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanity-lnet test_270 - Didn't find metadata reply message in log
item.summary: IPv6 sanity-lnet test_270: FAIL: Didn't find metadata reply message in log

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: 7 records found on master branch in the past 4 weeks.

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61672/
Subject: LU-19363 kefalnd: fix build after header move
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: b1770c31a82679e55231105ad98593d372d4d815

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61673
Subject: LU-19363 kefalnd: enable kefalnd if headers can be found
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 1a215c0c9dc194523088b97b9b98d45c0675650c

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61672
Subject: LU-19363 kefalnd: fix build after header move
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 84fe8ef588a459201aef323f87326802392d1eb5

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61671
Subject: LU-19363 kefalnd: rework ping code to be clearer
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: f561e6bbde32329dc01884e31b0046053ec45bae

Tim, it is clear from looking at the patch again that this "peer metadata" functionality is broken for IPv6 NIDs needs at a minimum some code changes to handle this properly, if not protocol change to work with large NIDs.

My understanding was that the "fetch metadata" functionality in LU-18808 was supposed to be generic enough that other LNDs can use it, and sanity-lnet test_270 was added with that expectation that this is working properly for TCP. This isn't just a question of whether the current EFA LND does not need the large NID case, but whether EFA or some other LND would use this with large NIDs and it will be broken because the larger NID is clobbering some of the other data in the reply.

I now see in the patch that this network protocol message was added with a small NID at the start instead of reserving the few extra bytes necessary to allow a large NID:

struct lnet_nid_md_entry {
	lnet_nid_t			nid;
	__u8				buffer[LNET_MD_BUFFER_SZ];
} __attribute__((packed));

This seems like a terrible shortcoming at this point to be adding something that only allows IPv4 NIDs when we are working to enable IPv6 for upstream acceptance.

IFF you are already using this feature in production with the current message format, then it makes the most sense sense to change nid to be a union between struct lnet_nid and lnet_nid_t and then interpret struct lnet_nid.nid_size to dynamically determine the actual NID size and accesses the extra metadata in buffer with the correct offset.

What I don't understand is why the large NID is affecting num_nid_mappings instead of cutting into buffer in each of the struct lnet_nid_md_entry entries?

NB: please add lnme_ prefixes for these struct fields.

"Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61361
Subject: LU-19363 tests: skip sanity-lnet/270 for IPv6
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: ca8ea71df84ea39ebb8fe24e28138c9b13e8cb38

This is an expected failure. Currently, there's only a need to attach metadata to small NIDs (for EFA small NID -> GID resolution - see https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58407/comment/44f55e24_c944dbb3/). It's not difficult to attach metadata to large NIDs - but there isn't any use case for it right now - so there's no point.

 

The test is failure specifically because we are attempting to fetch metadata from a peer that doesn't have any (since it only has large NIDs). In the real world, this would never happen. The EFA LND will only call lnet_discover_nid_metadata() on peer that it definitely knows has metadata (i.e. it has a small EFA NID).

 

I can write a patch to skip this test in IPv6-only environment.

Marking Critical until we know if this is related to network protocol changes added in LU-18808 on not. If necessary, there is still have time in the 2.17.0 release window to change the network protocol for this LND discovery to handle IPv6 NIDs.

timday, can you (or someone from AWS) look at this other test failure that has been hit with the new test_270 added by patch https://review.whamcloud.com/58407 ("LU-18808 lnet: allow LNDs to discover metadata via pings") that has been drowned out by the frequent LU-16807 UBSAN failures.

It looks like this is failing 100% with review-dne-ipv6 test sessions:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?horizon=2332800&test_groups%5B%5D=review-dne-ipv6&test_set_script_id=a2b1c4b2-b449-11e9-b88c-52540065bddc&sub_test_script_id=d8881e24-80d2-4340-bc9b-ad64a26c0440&source=sub_tests#redirect

LNetError: 1514620:0:(api-ni.c:10644:lnet_discover_nid_metadata()) 12345-fd33:3981:3213:f020:0:5254:db:1bae@tcp: Unexpected number of handshake entries 363512030: rc = -71

This error was also added in the same patch as the test. I certainly hope that this new functionality is compatible with IPv6 NIDs and not assuming only IPv4 NIDs are in use? There is still time to fix this before the 2.17.0 release in that case.
